title: Inventory Level Monitoring and Alerting
description: The system shall monitor inventory levels for ABO/Rh blood types and raise alerts when the count drops below 5 units.
rationale: This requirement is necessary to ensure timely restocking of blood supplies, preventing shortages during high-demand periods.
business_value: By automatically raising alerts, the system will enable prompt action by inventory managers, thereby ensuring a consistent supply of blood to meet demand and improve patient care.

source.description: As an inventory manager, I need the system to monitor inventory levels and raise alerts when the count of any ABO/Rh type drops below 5 units so that I can restock blood supplies in time to meet demand.

acceptance_criteria: Given the current inventory for a specific ABO/Rh type is below 5 units
When the system checks inventory levels
Then an alert is raised for that blood type
postconditions: An alert is triggered for the specific ABO/Rh type with insufficient inventory
functional_constraints: The system must accurately track inventory levels by ABO/Rh type
The threshold for triggering an alert should be set at 5 units per type

non_functional_requirements.performance: Alerts must be raised within 10 seconds of the inventory check
non_functional_requirements.availability: 99.9% uptime of the monitoring system to ensure timely alerts during peak demand periods
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should handle up to 1,000 ABO/Rh types simultaneously without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Alerts should be easily distinguishable and accessible via email or dashboard notification

edge_cases: What happens if the inventory data is delayed by more than 10 seconds?
How does the system handle multiple alerts for different ABO/Rh types simultaneously?
What if there is a network outage during an alert trigger?

interfaces.name: Inventory API
interfaces.description: The system interacts with the Inventory API to retrieve current inventory levels for ABO/Rh types.

interfaces.name: Alert Notification System
interfaces.description: The system sends alerts via the Alert Notification System, which could be an email service or a dashboard application.

test_cases.description: Check inventory levels for a type with 4 units and verify no alert is triggered.
test_cases.expected_result: No alert should be raised as the inventory level is above the threshold.

test_cases.description: Check inventory levels for a type with 5 units and verify no alert is triggered.
test_cases.expected_result: No alert should be raised as the inventory level equals the threshold.

test_cases.description: Check inventory levels for a type with 4.9 units and verify an alert is triggered.
test_cases.expected_result: An alert should be raised as the inventory level is below the threshold.

test_cases.description: Simulate a network outage during an alert trigger and verify the system handles it gracefully.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should not trigger an alert but log the error and retry after network stability.
